About Me

Precision Engineer.
Spatial Thinker.

8+
Years in Geomatics

I am Er. Prem Prakash Khadka, a professional Geomatics Engineer graduated from Kathmandu University, registered as a General Engineer with the Nepal Engineering Council (NEC).

Currently serving as a Government Surveyor at the Survey Office Nawalparasi, I specialize in high-precision cadastral surveying, LiDAR data capture, topographic mapping, and integrating complex spatial data to solve real-world problems across Nepal's diverse terrain — from terai plains to Himalayan highlands.

Beyond traditional surveying, my expertise extends to disaster risk reduction, WebGIS development, UAV photogrammetry, and environmental modeling — leveraging advanced geoprocessing to build safer, more resilient communities.

Quick Facts

Built a browser-based Traverse Computation Suite used by surveyors across Nepal.
Passionate trekker who combines fieldwork with personal exploration of Nepal's landscapes.

What I Do

Core Competencies

Comprehensive geospatial services bridging field data, digital mapping, and actionable intelligence.

Land & Engineering Surveying

Expertise in cadastral bounds, topographic surveys, and highway alignment using high-precision GNSS and Total Stations.

GIS & Spatial Analysis

Advanced multi-criteria decision making (AHP), network analysis, and spatial modeling for urban planning and disaster management.

LiDAR Surveying

High-density point cloud data collection, classification, and DTM/DSM generation for precision mapping and infrastructure design.

UAV Photogrammetry

Drone flight planning, image processing, and generation of high-resolution orthomosaics, DEMs, and 3D point clouds.

My Work

Projects & Research

Academic research, hazard modeling frameworks, and custom surveying software tools.

Live Web App

Traverse Computation Suite

JavaScript Surveying Math Web Tool Bowditch Rule

A custom client-side web application designed to automate complex surveying mathematics, allowing surveyors to process field data directly in the browser — no installation required.

  • Closed Loop & Link Traverses: Automatically distributes angular misclosures and applies the Bowditch (compass) rule for linear error adjustment.
  • Trigonometric Levelling: Computes elevation traverses using forward and back zenithal angle observations.
  • Interactive UI: Generates dynamic input tables and provides clear, export-ready reporting of calculated coordinates and reduced levels.
Launch Application

Multi-Hazard Risk Assessment of Dhading District

ArcGIS Pro AHP Hazard Modeling Remote Sensing

A comprehensive multi-hazard risk assessment integrating physical hazard data with socio-economic vulnerability metrics across Dhading District.

  • Mapped susceptibility for floods, landslides, and forest fires using over 16 conditioning factors.
  • Developed a Socio-Economic Vulnerability Index (SEVI) using National Census data.
  • Applied the Analytical Hierarchy Process (AHP) in ArcGIS achieving highly consistent weight matrices.
View Research Report

Soil Erosion Estimation — Dhading (RUSLE Model)

RUSLE Model Spatial Analysis Environmental Monitoring

An empirical model to quantify spatial soil loss patterns in Dhading District, supporting agricultural and conservation planning decisions.

  • Calculated primary RUSLE factors: Rainfall erosivity (R), Soil erodibility (K), Topographic parameters (LS), and Cover management (C).
  • Estimated a total annual mean soil loss of 10.4 million tonnes across the district.
  • Categorized the district into erosion severity classes to identify critical conservation zones.
View Research Report
Field & Campus Life

Photo Gallery

A visual journey through field surveys, academic milestones, and memorable moments from Nepal's diverse landscapes.

LiDAR Survey
Mewa Khola, Taplejung
Airborne LiDAR data acquisition in the remote Mewa Khola valley, Taplejung — high-altitude point-cloud capture for precision terrain modeling.
Field Survey
Traverse Survey — Naysham Khola
Total station traverse survey deep inside the forest of Naysham Khola, collecting ground control points for topographic mapping.
Project Defense
Multi-Hazard Risk Assessment — Dhading
Final year project defense for the multi-hazard risk assessment of Dhading District at Kathmandu University, covering floods, landslides & forest fires.
Project Defense
Soil Erosion Assessment — RUSLE Method
Presentation of the soil erosion estimation study for Dhading District using the Revised Universal Soil Loss Equation (RUSLE) at Kathmandu University.

A meaningful visit to the sacred Palanchoki Temple — combining cultural heritage with the spirit of exploration shared with colleagues and friends.

Academic field survey conducted in Chitang as part of the Kathmandu University Geomatics Engineering curriculum — total station traversing with the student team.
